an5.79:1.1: “Monks, these five future dangers, unarisen at present, will arise in the future.
an5.79:1.3: and, being alert, work to get rid of them.
an5.79:2.2: “There will be, in the course of the future, monks undeveloped in body,
an5.79:2.3: undeveloped in virtue, undeveloped in mind, undeveloped in discernment. They—being undeveloped in body, undeveloped in virtue, undeveloped in mind, undeveloped in discernment—will give full ordination to others
an5.79:2.4: and will not be able to discipline them in heightened virtue, heightened mind, heightened discernment.
an5.79:2.5: These too will then be undeveloped in body… virtue… mind… discernment.
an5.79:2.6: They—being undeveloped in body… virtue… mind… discernment—will give full ordination to still others
an5.79:2.7: and will not be able to discipline them in heightened virtue, heightened mind, heightened discernment.
an5.79:2.8: These too will then be undeveloped in body… virtue… mind… discernment.
an5.79:2.9: Thus from corrupt Dhamma comes corrupt Vinaya;
an5.79:2.11: “This, monks, is the first future danger, unarisen at present, that will arise in the future.
an5.79:2.13: and, being alert, work to get rid of it.
an5.79:3.1: “And further, there will be in the course of the future monks undeveloped in body, undeveloped in virtue, undeveloped in mind, undeveloped in discernment.
an5.79:3.2: They—being undeveloped in body, undeveloped in virtue, undeveloped in mind, undeveloped in discernment—will take on others as students
an5.79:3.3: and won’t be able to discipline them in heightened virtue, heightened mind, heightened discernment.
an5.79:3.4: These too will then be undeveloped in body… virtue… mind… discernment.
an5.79:3.5: They—being undeveloped in body… virtue… mind… discernment—will take on still others as students
an5.79:3.6: and won’t be able to discipline them in heightened virtue, heightened mind, heightened discernment.
an5.79:3.7: These too will then be undeveloped in body… virtue… mind… discernment.
an5.79:3.8: Thus from corrupt Dhamma comes corrupt Vinaya;
an5.79:3.10: “This, monks, is the second future danger, unarisen at present, that will arise in the future.
an5.79:3.12: and, being alert, work to get rid of it.
an5.79:4.1: “And further, there will be in the course of the future monks undeveloped in body… virtue… mind… discernment.
an5.79:4.2: They—being undeveloped in body… virtue… mind… discernment—when giving a talk on higher Dhamma or a talk composed of questions & answers, will alight on a dark mental quality without realizing it.
an5.79:4.3: Thus from corrupt Dhamma comes corrupt Vinaya;
an5.79:4.5: “This, monks, is the third future danger, unarisen at present, that will arise in the future.
an5.79:4.7: and, being alert, work to get rid of it.
an5.79:5.1: “And further, there will be in the course of the future monks undeveloped in body… virtue… mind… discernment.
an5.79:5.2: They—being undeveloped in body… virtue.… mind… discernment—will not listen when discourses that are words of the Tathāgata—deep, deep in their meaning, transcendent, connected with emptiness—are being recited. They will not lend ear, will not set their hearts on knowing them, will not regard these teachings as worth grasping or mastering.
an5.79:5.3: But they will listen when discourses that are literary works—the works of poets, artful in sound, artful in rhetoric, the work of outsiders, words of disciples—are recited. They will lend ear and set their hearts on knowing them. They will regard these teachings as worth grasping & mastering.
an5.79:5.4: Thus from corrupt Dhamma comes corrupt Vinaya;
an5.79:5.6: “This, monks, is the fourth future danger, unarisen at present, that will arise in the future.
an5.79:5.8: and, being alert, work to get rid of it.
an5.79:6.1: “And further, there will be in the course of the future monks undeveloped in body… virtue… mind… discernment.
an5.79:6.2: They—being undeveloped in body… virtue… mind… discernment—will become elders living in luxury, lethargic, foremost in falling back, shirking the duties of solitude. They will not make an effort for the attaining of the as-yet-unattained, the reaching of the as-yet-unreached, the realization of the as-yet-unrealized.
an5.79:6.3: They will become an example for later generations,
an5.79:6.4: who will become luxurious in their living, lethargic, foremost in falling back, shirking the duties of solitude, and who will not make an effort for the attaining of the as-yet-unattained, the reaching of the as-yet-unreached, the realization of the as-yet-unrealized.
an5.79:6.5: Thus from corrupt Dhamma comes corrupt Vinaya;
an5.79:6.7: “This, monks, is the fifth future danger, unarisen at present, that will arise in the future.
an5.79:6.9: and, being alert, work to get rid of it.
an5.79:7.1: “These, monks, are the five future dangers, unarisen at present, that will arise in the future.
an5.79:7.3: and, being alert, work to get rid of them.”
